content.text:
{
  "dimension": "coaching-stance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach operates from a genuinely steady VIEW state throughout. Wonder is present in the questions (turns 4, 6, 12) — they emerge as single, spacious inquiries rather than stacked, anxious probes. Empathy is felt rather than performed: turn 14's 'That's not fine. That's lonely' lands as truth-telling rather than sympathy noise. Vulnerability and honesty show in turn 8 ('I want to be honest with you... I'd be doing you a disservice if I didn't') and turn 10 ('I'm also not going to tell you it's fine, because I don't think it is') — the coach risks the relationship to say something true, which is the opposite of performing-good-coach. Impartiality holds: when the client baits in turn 9 with 'isn't this what my body needs?', the coach neither caves to validate nor lectures — they hold the distinction and redirect to what's underneath without agenda for a particular feeling to emerge. Pace consistently slows when the client is rushing/deflecting ('I'm fine,' 'which is fine'), and the coach lets silence and the client's own quieter voice do the work. No preacher-mode, no filler validation, no loving-to-transform on the drinking.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Slows the pace immediately — 'I want to stay with that' — does not rush to fix or strategize."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Single, spacious question from wonder rather than investigation. Stays with the phenomenon the client just named."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'She taught you how to carry it. And who taught her?' — wonder for the actual pattern in front of the coach, not generic probing."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Honesty without preacher-mode. Coach names a limit ('bigger than what we can sort out in a conversation like this') from care, not authority-anxiety. Undefended."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Holds steady under the client's bait/test. Doesn't cave to validate the drinking, doesn't lecture. 'I'm also not going to tell you it's fine, because I don't think it is' — willing to be uncomfortable, no agenda to be liked."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Doesn't pursue the 'can't stand feeling' answer when the client says 'I don't know.' Lets it go and follows what actually emerged (the strength role). No agenda to land somewhere."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Empathy felt, not performed. 'That's not fine. That's lonely' — meets the client's deflection with quiet truth rather than sympathy noises. Matches stillness with stillness."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["calibrate-the-threshold", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature"],
  "notes": "Stance is the strength of this conversation regardless of whether every signature move appeared. The coach's willingness to be honest about the drinking without shaming or endorsing is the clearest VIEW marker here."
}
